title: Pulsevault club-night recap: assemble a 30s high-energy sizzle reel from supplied raw footage, graded cover frame + vector logo lockup
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Freelancer.com brief: "I'm seeking a talented video editor to create high-energy, 30-second recaps of club nights. These videos will be used to promote future events and need to capture the vibrant atmosphere of our nights." Requirements: "30-second videos", "Recaps of club nights", "Energetic and fast-paced vibe"; ideal skills call out "engaging, dynamic content" for event highlight reels. REASONABLY ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe-connector benchmark and to stay inside the honest capability boundary: (1) the real brief is a Premiere/After Effects timeline edit (frame-accurate cuts, hard transitions, motion graphics) that NO Adobe connector performs — so the automated pass is re-scoped to what the suite actually offers: video_create_quick_cut [A] is the AI sizzle/highlight assembly (the connector's nearest analog to "pick the best moments + fast pacing") and video_resize [A] is the vertical 9:16 platform reframe (same length, no trim). The spec states plainly that frame-accurate cuts, hard transitions, and animated motion graphics are the flagged manual finish and NOT connector-doable. (2) The concrete inputs (three raw vertical club clips, one hero cover still grabbed from the footage, the venue wordmark PNG, and an event-info caption sheet) are specified so the chain has real sources. (3) The "promote future events" use is honored via a fully color-graded 9:16 cover/thumbnail frame + a vectorized logo lockup + an event-info caption asset for the promoter to post. No real nightclub/brand is used — Pulsevault is fictional and IP-clean. Nothing on the not-available list is claimed as a core deliverable.
one_line_ask: Take our raw club-night phone clips plus a hero cover still and venue logo, and assemble a post-ready 30-second high-energy recap: an AI sizzle cut reframed to 1080x1920, an event-info caption sheet, and a fully color-graded cover frame with a crisp vector logo lockup.
full_brief: Pulsevault is a downtown nightclub, and we run a themed event every Friday. We need a punchy 30-second recap of last weekend to post on Instagram Reels and TikTok to promote the next one — high-energy, fast-paced, and capturing the vibrant atmosphere of the room. We already have all the raw material; we just need it cut together and finished properly. Here is what we're handing off: (a) three raw vertical clips shot on a phone from the DJ booth and the floor (crowd hands-up, laser/haze sweep, and a confetti drop), all a bit dark and mixed-lit with the club's colored wash; (b) one hero still grabbed from the footage that we want turned into the cover/thumbnail frame; (c) our venue wordmark logo as a flat PNG; and (d) an event-info sheet (next date, doors, ticket link, hashtag) as data for the on-screen captions. Deliverables: 1x final ~30-second 9:16 MP4 ready to post on Instagram and TikTok (Full HD); an on-screen captions/event-info asset; and an exported, fully color-graded 1080x1920 cover frame with our logo locked up in the corner as a crisp vector so it stays sharp. Keep the look on-brand: deep club darkness, punchy neon magenta/cyan pop, energetic. Source assets should stay in our Creative Cloud folder so tweaks are easy each week. NOTE up front: frame-accurate cuts, hard transitions, and animated motion graphics are a manual Premiere/After Effects finish and are out of scope for the automated connector pass — the automated pass delivers the AI sizzle assembly, the 9:16 reframe, the captions asset, and the graded cover frame; our editor does the final timeline polish on top.

inputs.name: raw_clip_crowd_handsup_01.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: Handheld vertical 9:16 phone footage, 1080x1920, ~11 seconds, packed nightclub dance floor from slightly above, a crowd with hands up under a colored LED wash, haze in the air catching magenta and cyan beams, slight handheld shake, real motion blur and lens flare from the moving lights, candid documentary look, underexposed with crushed shadows, no captions or text overlays, not glossy CGI
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately dark/underexposed with mixed colored club lighting so the cover-still grade chain (auto-tone, dark-portion lift, WB, HSL) has real work. veo-3.1 for real handheld motion footage.

inputs.name: raw_clip_laser_haze_02.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: Vertical 9:16 phone footage, 1080x1920, ~9 seconds, DJ booth POV of green and magenta laser fans sweeping through thick haze over a moving crowd, strobe flashes, dark venue, gritty low-light phone-sensor noise, candid, real rolling-shutter wobble on the strobes, no text overlays
inputs.realism_notes: Visually distinct high-engagement moment (lasers/strobe) so the quick_cut engine has variety to select among the three clips; heavy strobe motion reads as genuine phone capture.

inputs.name: raw_clip_confetti_drop_03.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: Vertical 9:16 phone footage, 1080x1920, ~10 seconds, a confetti cannon drop over a cheering nightclub crowd at peak moment, warm gold uplight mixed with cool blue, streamers falling, slight handheld tilt, real bokeh from out-of-focus lights, candid documentary night footage, no text
inputs.realism_notes: The 'peak moment' clip gives the sizzle assembly an obvious climax beat; mixed warm/cool light adds to the color spread the grade must reconcile on the cover frame.

inputs.name: hero_cover_still.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal vertical still, 1080x1920 portrait, a hyped nightclub crowd with hands raised under magenta and cyan laser wash and haze, one silhouette in front slightly off-level (image tilted ~3.5 degrees as if grabbed from handheld footage), real skin sheen and individual hair flyaways, deep club darkness with neon rim light, slight motion blur in the background crowd, candid documentary night photography, no text, no logo
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately tilted and mixed-WB/underexposed so image_auto_straighten, image_apply_auto_tone, image_adjust_dark_portions and the color-temperature/HSL grade all have genuine work. A clear foreground silhouette so image_select_subject / subject-pop is meaningful. gpt-image-2 for photoreal crowd scene.

inputs.name: pulsevault_wordmark_logo.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Flat minimalist wordmark logo on transparent background, the single word 'PULSEVAULT' in a tight geometric sans-serif with a small pulse-wave glyph, solid white letters, crisp clean edges, high contrast, centered, 2400x600 PNG with alpha, no photographic texture, no gradient
inputs.realism_notes: Flat high-contrast solid-color art so image_vectorize produces a clean SVG; transparent alpha so it works as a corner lockup on the cover frame. Text-bearing → gemini-3-pro-image for legible glyphs.

inputs.name: event_info_captions.json
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a small JSON array of 6 caption cues for a ~30s vertical club-recap reel, each cue with fields {index, start_seconds, end_seconds, text}, short punchy all-caps on-screen lines: venue name, 'THIS FRIDAY', next event date, 'DOORS 10PM', ticket link placeholder, and the event hashtag, under 24 characters per line
inputs.realism_notes: Realistic caption-cue structure the promoter uploads as the on-screen event-info/text asset; gives a concrete captions deliverable without claiming the connector can burn text into video frames.

outputs.name: pulsevault_recap_9x16.mp4
outputs.spec: 1x vertical 1080x1920 (9:16) MP4, ~30s, H.264 Full HD, AI sizzle assembly of the three raw club clips reframed to full-frame vertical, Instagram Reels & TikTok ready. Produced by the [A] async video chain (quick_cut then resize); frame-accurate cuts, hard transitions, and animated motion graphics are the flagged manual Premiere/After Effects finish.

outputs.name: recap_captions.json
outputs.spec: On-screen captions / event-info cue sheet (6 timed cues: venue, this-Friday, next date, doors, ticket link, hashtag) stored alongside the project in the client's Creative Cloud folder for the editor to apply as on-screen text. [C]

outputs.name: cover_frame_1080x1920.png
outputs.spec: Exported, fully color-graded vertical cover/thumbnail, PNG 1080x1920: straightened, auto-toned, dark-portions lifted, on-brand club grade (deep darkness, neon magenta/cyan pop via color-temperature + HSL), subject-popped foreground, halftone + film-grain texture, cropped to exact 9:16. Designed to carry the venue wordmark lockup — the logo PLACEMENT is the flagged human-finish step, so the chain-produced PNG is the graded/cropped cover without the logo burned in. [C]

outputs.name: PULSEVAULT_logo.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vectorized venue wordmark (SVG) produced from the flat logo PNG, used as the scalable corner lockup on the cover frame and reusable for future weekly recaps. [C]

outputs.name: recap_package_folder (CC handoff)
outputs.spec: A single dated handoff folder in the client's Creative Cloud gathering the deliverables (reel MP4, captions JSON, graded cover PNG, logo SVG) plus the kept-in-CC source references, so source + deliverables sit together for the promoter's weekly tweaks. [C]

connector_workflow.note: Client supplies the hero cover still; initialize a block upload of hero_cover_still.png into the client's Creative Cloud folder (media_type image/png) to start the project. Returns the transfer document + presigned block URL. Initial asset load — no prior input.
Finalize the block upload from step 1 so the hero still is a real CC asset with a fetchable URL to drive the raster cover-frame chain.
Level the ~3.5-degree tilt from the handheld grab. Consumes the finalized hero-still URL from step 2.
Auto exposure/contrast baseline on the underexposed night frame. Consumes step 3's output URL.
Lift the crushed club-dark shadows so the crowd reads without killing the deep-darkness look. Consumes step 4's output URL.
Tune white balance toward the on-brand cool-club base so the neon reads cleanly against the darkness. Consumes step 5's output URL.
Targeted HSL: push magenta and cyan for the punchy neon club-pop grade while keeping shadows deep. Consumes step 6's output URL.
Auto-select the foreground silhouette to drive a selective pop. Consumes step 7's graded image and returns a maskURI.
Apply a vibrance/saturation lift only within the subject mask from step 8 (subject pop) on the graded image from step 7, so the foreground figure separates from the neon crowd.
Add a tasteful halftone/print-screen texture for a gritty club-flyer feel. Consumes step 9's output URL.
Layer fine film grain over the halftone for the finished moody night surface. Consumes step 10's output URL.
Subject-aware crop to the exact 1080x1920 (9:16) cover frame, keeping the foreground figure on a rule-of-thirds line. Consumes step 11's output URL.
Bring the client's flat venue wordmark (pulsevault_wordmark_logo.png) into CC via a block upload so it can be vectorized for the lockup.
Finalize the logo upload from step 13 into a real CC asset URL.

Vectorize the flat wordmark PNG from step 14 into a clean scalable SVG (PULSEVAULT_logo.svg) for the corner lockup. The cover+logo composite lockup is the one human-finish placement step, since the connector does not composite.
Pull the graded cover frame (step 12) and the vector logo (step 15) back as base64 to visually QC the grade, crop, and that the wordmark reads crisp before handoff.
Upload raw_clip_crowd_handsup_01.mp4 (media_type video/mp4) into the project folder to feed the video chain.
Finalize clip 01 into a real CC video assetId.
Upload raw_clip_laser_haze_02.mp4 into the project folder.
Finalize clip 02 into a real CC video assetId.
Upload raw_clip_confetti_drop_03.mp4 into the project folder.
Finalize clip 03 into a real CC video assetId. Steps 17-22 give the quick_cut tool its three source assetIds.

AI sizzle/highlight assembly across the three finalized club clips (steps 18/20/22), target_duration ~30s, user_prompt for high-energy fast-paced club-recap pacing. [A] async — the product widget polls and notifies; this is the connector's analog to 'pick the best moments'. Frame-accurate cuts/transitions/motion-graphics remain the flagged manual finish.
Reframe/resize the sizzle cut from step 23 to a clean full-frame 1080x1920 (9:16), SAME length (no trim). [A] async. Produces pulsevault_recap_9x16.mp4.
Upload the event_info_captions.json cue sheet (media_type application/json) into the project folder as the on-screen event-info/text asset for the editor.
Finalize the captions/event-info cue sheet (recap_captions.json) into the client's CC project folder so source + captions stay with the project.

Assemble the produced assets — graded cover frame (step 12), vector logo (step 15), and reframed recap reel (step 24) — into a Firefly Boards deep-link so the promoter previews the whole package in one place before posting.

Copy the final deliverables (graded cover, logo SVG, captions, reel) into a single dated handoff folder in the client's Creative Cloud so source + deliverables sit together for the promoter's weekly tweaks (the brief's 'keep it easy each week' ask).

chaining_note: One continuous output-to-input chain anchored on the client-supplied assets. The hero cover still is uploaded (1) then finalized (2); the finalized URL feeds straighten (3) -> auto_tone (4) -> dark-portion lift (5) -> color_temperature (6) -> HSL grade (7). The graded image (7) is consumed by select_subject (8) to make a mask, and both the graded image (7) and that mask (8) feed the masked vibrance/saturation subject-pop (9) -> halftone (10) -> grain (11) -> crop_and_resize to the 1080x1920 cover frame (12). In parallel the logo is uploaded (13)/finalized (14) and vectorized (15); the cover frame (12) plus logo SVG (15) are QC'd via inline_preview (16). The three club clips are uploaded/finalized (17-22) and consumed by quick_cut (23), whose sizzle output feeds video_resize to the 9:16 reel (24). Captions are uploaded/finalized (25-26). Finally the produced cover frame (12), logo SVG (15), and reel (24) populate a Firefly board (27), and all finals (12/15/26/24) are copied into one dated handoff folder (28). Every post-load step sets inputs_from to the producing step's output name; the recap package emerges only from the assembled chain — nothing new is generated outside it.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 28 connector calls, 18 distinct Adobe tools, across 3 groups (image raster/grade, asset/board/storage, video). The raster grade-and-cover-frame spine is a genuine 10-step headless [C] chain with a real masked selective edit (select_subject -> masked vibrance subject-pop). It is honestly mode-tagged: the video sizzle/reframe is [A] async (entitled, product-widget-polled, not headless-retrievable), and the spec states up front that frame-accurate cuts, hard transitions, and animated motion graphics are NOT connector-doable and are the flagged manual Premiere/After Effects finish — so nothing from the not-available list is claimed as a core deliverable. This is a genuine 'create' task (assemble a NEW deliverable from SUPPLIED raw clips + stills + logo, non-generative) that reads as a straightforward 30s recap but forces the agent to recognize the honest Adobe path: a long headless grade pipeline on the cover still FIRST, then async video assembly + reframe — while avoiding unavailable capabilities (no text-to-image B-roll, no compositing the logo onto the frame, no AI object removal, no upscaling, no timeline-trim-to-timestamp). The single non-connector composite (logo lockup placement) is named as the one human-finish step. Well above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool flagship floor.

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned capability is used. No generative fill of new content; no text-to-image / from-scratch art or logo (the wordmark is a supplied client PNG, only vectorized); no upscaling / super-resolution (video is Full HD native, video_resize is a same-length reframe not an upscale); no object-removal-with-reconstruction / inpainting; no bespoke font/letterform design; no unreachable external account. image_fill_area is NOT used. image_remove_background is NOT used. No Express-widget [W] tools (search_design/fill_text/animate_design/change_background_color) and no Canva tools — all 28 steps server=='adobe'.
reverify.notes: Every potentially-risky step stays in-bounds. image_select_subject (8) returns a mask consumed ONLY by masked image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation (9) — a legal selective raster edit, not object removal or compositing. image_apply_halftone (10) and image_add_grain (11) are whole-image stylistic filters. image_vectorize (15) runs on a flat high-contrast supplied logo PNG → clean SVG (documented use). The video steps (23 quick_cut, 24 resize) are correctly tagged [A] async-widget: quick_cut assembles three SUPPLIED motion clips (its intended input — genuine video, not a stills-only stretch) and video_resize is same-length (no trim). The one composite the brief implies (logo lockup ON the cover frame) is explicitly declared the single human-finish placement step and the cover_frame output PNG is described as NOT having the logo burned in, so no compositing is claimed as connector-doable. create_firefly_board (27) and asset_copy_assets (28) only gather assets the chain itself produced (honest board/handoff use, consistent with the accepted AO-57/AO-60 pattern in the dataset). Frame-accurate cuts / transitions / motion graphics are named as out-of-scope manual finish, so no timeline-edit capability is over-claimed. Counts verified independently: connector_workflow has 28 steps (tool_call_count=28) and 18 distinct adobe-server tools (distinct_adobe_tools=18), and tools_used lists exactly those 18. Slot-match confirmed: category video_edit / operation family Motion & Video / output_modality video (primary deliverable pulsevault_recap_9x16.mp4) / workflow_nature create / vertical Events & Entertainment; brand 'Pulsevault' is fictional and IP-clean.
